description: Corporate legal requirements for Singapore companies - AGM, EGM, filing deadlines

rules.title: Annual General Meeting (AGM) Requirements
rules.description: Singapore companies must hold an AGM within 6 months from the end of their financial year
requirements: AGM must be held once every calendar year
First AGM must be held within 18 months of incorporation
Gap between AGMs cannot exceed 15 months
Notice of AGM must be given at least 14 days before the meeting (21 days for listed companies)
Financial statements must be laid before AGM
rules.deadline_calculation: Within 6 months from the end of financial year
consequences: Directors may be fined up to $10,000

solely_exempt_private_company.description: Solely Exempt Private Companies (SEPC) are exempt from holding AGMs
conditions: Company has only individual shareholders (no corporate shareholders)
Company is not a sole director company

rules.title: Extraordinary General Meeting (EGM) Requirements
rules.description: Meetings called for special business that cannot wait until the next AGM
trigger_conditions: Any urgent business requiring shareholder approval
Major transactions under Section 160 of Companies Act
rules.notice_period: 14 days notice (21 days for listed companies)
rules.quorum: At least 2 shareholders present (unless constitution states otherwise)

rules.title: Annual Return Filing Requirements
rules.description: ACRA Annual Return must be filed within 7 days after AGM (or 7 months from FYE for exemption)
rules.deadline: 7 days after AGM is held (or within 7 months from FYE for exempt companies)

penalties.late_filing: $300 for first 1-60 days, $600 for 61-180 days, additional penalties up to $2,000

rules.title: Financial Year End (FYE) Rules
rules.description: Companies can choose any financial year end date
considerations: FYE should align with business cycle
First FY cannot exceed 18 months from incorporation
Most companies use calendar year end (31 Dec) or 31 March
Group companies should align FYE for consolidated accounts

rules.title: Required Registers and Records
rules.description: Companies must maintain various statutory registers
required_registers: Minute Books of Board and General Meetings

rules.title: Director Requirements
requirements: At least 1 director who is ordinarily resident in Singapore
No disqualification under Companies Act Section 149
Must not be an undischarged bankrupt
rules.resignation: Director can resign by giving notice to company. Company must file resignation with ACRA within 14 days

rules.title: Company Secretary Requirements
requirements: At least 1 secretary for every company
Secretary must be appointed within 6 months of incorporation
For listed companies, secretary must have at least 3 years experience
Secretary must be ordinarily resident in Singapore
duties: File annual returns and other statutory filings

rules.title: Registered Office Address Requirements
rules.description: Company must have a registered office in Singapore
requirements: Must be an address in Singapore (cannot be PO Box)
Must be open to public during business hours
Must receive and forward official correspondence

rules.title: Tax Filing Requirements
rules.description: IRAS tax filing obligations for companies

forms.deadline: 30 November of the year following YA

source: Companies Act (Chapter 50), ACRA Practice Directions, IRAS Guidelines
